proposals.evidence: Citation pattern shows consistent use of ¶ in all other ND citations (2008 ND 89, ¶ 15; 2015 ND 233, ¶ 6; etc.). Only this citation lacks the ¶. Visual inspection of PDF page 3 confirms curly quotes and the ¶ symbol before '3'. Source text from pdftotext shows: 'C.A.S., 2023 ND 122, ¶ 3, 993 N.W.2d 347'.
proposals.note: OCR/ingestion artifact: the ¶ symbol (U+00B6) was dropped from this single citation while all other paragraph citations in the opinion retain it correctly.

coverage_note: Comprehensive review completed. Text content is 99.59% identical to source; remaining differences are layout, formatting, and character encoding (straight vs. curly quotes/apostrophes) which are systematic OCR artifacts. One substantive transcription error identified: missing paragraph symbol (¶) in a single citation. The opinion text otherwise shows no split/join errors, missing lines, or other transcription defects."

The PDF source shows a form feed followed immediately by the running header; the filing header is layout-only on page 1.
